message:
refactor(tui): rename ftui_stub → ftui_backend to match reality (ft-lnlg4)

The file was scaffolded as an empty placeholder, but the 8296-line body
is now the production FrankenTUI backend — Home, Panes, Search, Help,
Events, Triage, History, Timeline views all backed by live state.

The `_stub` suffix in the module name is pure legacy drift. Every
reality-check sweep over this crate flags `ftui_stub` as a candidate
for deletion/investigation; reviewers then burn cycles confirming it's
a false positive. Name should follow reality.

Changes:
- git mv crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs
        crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_backend.rs
  (preserves blame/history).
- mod.rs: `mod ftui_stub;` → `mod ftui_backend;`; two re-export lines
  updated (`pub use ftui_stub::{...}` → `pub use ftui_backend::{...}`);
  added a short breadcrumb comment pointing at ft-lnlg4 so future
  grep-ers who search for the old name land on the rename record.
- rollout.rs: three references updated
  (pub use super::ftui_stub::{View, ViewState}; and two call sites
  inside the TuiBackend::Ftui match arm).
- keymap.rs: stale doc-comment reference ftui_stub.rs → ftui_backend.rs.

No behavioral change — pure rename + doc alignment. Git mv preserves
file blame; re-exports keep the public API surface byte-identical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -98,7 +98,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-l6v1r","title":"[mor:distributed-systems] missed PaneDestroyed leaks tier/state maps across the swarm","description":"# Observation

`PaneTierClassifier` holds `RwLock<HashMap<u64, PaneState>>`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/pane_tiers.rs:238). Entries are added in
`register_pane` and removed in `unregister_pane`. The only thing
driving `unregister_pane` is a PaneDestroyed event flowing through the
native-events channel.

That channel has three failure modes documented in
`dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_cx`:
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Sent` — happy path
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Backpressure` — timeout expired, event dropped
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Closed` — channel gone

Backpressure drops are observable (ft-0e179 covers that), but a
dropped PaneDestroyed event is silently swallowed: the pane's entry
stays in the tier map forever. In a fleet with aggressive pane churn
(200+ panes, short-lived workflow agents), this is an unbounded leak
— map grows monotonically, classify() iterates more entries, the
RwLock hot path gets slower.

# Question

Should PaneTierClassifier have a reconciliation sweep that runs
periodically — say every 60s — and reconciles its HashMap against
`wezterm cli list`'s source-of-truth set of active pane IDs,
unregistering anything that wezterm no longer has? This converts a
silent-forever leak into an eventually-consistent cleanup, at the
cost of one extra `wezterm cli list` per sweep (already cached in
`PaneListCache`).

Same leak shape likely exists in other per-pane maps
(backpressure.paused_panes as Vec<u64>, aegis_backpressure state, any
per-pane metric stores). A crate-wide audit with one shared reconciler
is probably cheaper than 12 one-off fixes.

# CAP stance

Current design is "consistency by event delivery." On paper, the
event stream is authoritative. In practice, bounded queues mean
delivery is not guaranteed. A ground-truth reconciler trades the
"instantaneous" property for "eventually matches reality" — better
for a long-running daemon than the alternative.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:51.772093Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:30.788429Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:30.788109Z","close_reason":"Verified in d13fe125","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed-systems","mor","pane-lifecycle"]}
 {"id":"ft-l9mxa","title":"DistributedHttpClient response-read path does not observe mid-flight cx-cancel","description":"# DistributedHttpClient mid-flight cx-cancel gap

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 verification work. When a caller fires
`cx.cancel_with(...)` WHILE a `DistributedHttpClient::get` is in flight (blocked
reading response bytes from a stalled server), the cancel does NOT propagate to
the HTTP client's read loop.

## Reproduction
The ft-xbnl0.2.4 test
`distributed::tests::distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`
exercises this. Observed behavior:

    elapsed = 10.02s
    result = Err("deadline has elapsed at Time(10000000041ns)")

The elapsed = ~10s matches the test's DEFENSIVE outer timeout ceiling
(`crate::runtime_compat::timeout(10s, client.get(...))`), not the 50 ms
cx-cancel fire. The cancel went through (cx.is_cancel_requested() would return
true) but the HTTP client's await points didn't observe it.

## Relation to other findings
- **Tick 328** documented that `timeout_with_cx` observes cx *budget deadline*
  but not direct cx *cancel*. This is the same shape of gap at a different
  layer — the HTTP client wraps asupersync::http::h1 whose await points
  don't poll cx.
- **ft-kfkyi** (closed tick 351) was a distinct concern (3xx redirect
  exfil). This finding is lower-severity: not a security issue, just an
  operational-ergonomics gap.

## Impact
- Hung distributed RPC calls will eventually time out via internal limits
  (if any configured) but won't observe operator-initiated cancel.
- Agent workflows that fire cx.cancel to abort a slow peer call will wait
  the full internal-timeout window before the call returns.

## Fix approach (candidate paths)
1. Extend `DistributedHttpClient::get/post` to race the inner future against
   a cx-cancel future (tokio::select! or similar race).
2. Patch asupersync::http::h1 to thread cx through its await points and
   honor cancel at read/write boundaries. Bigger scope; upstream change.
3. Wrap the DistributedHttpClient inner calls in `timeout_with_cx` —
   but per tick 328, that only observes budget, not cancel. So this
   doesn't help unless the caller sets a short budget.

Option (1) is the narrowest change; aligns with the cx-first migration
pattern and doesn't require upstream asupersync work.

## Verification
The existing tick-380 snapshot test is the flip trigger. When the fix lands:
1. Observed elapsed drops from ~10s to << 10s (cx-cancel observed mid-read).
2. `result` shape changes from the outer-timeout Err to the HTTP client's
   own cancel-shaped Err.
3. Update the snapshot assertions to require the faster behavior.

## References
- Finding: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 (commit 4d925607)
- Related: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5` out-of-scope carve-out
  for primitive-doc-only coverage (timeout_with_cx / sleep_with_cx — ticks
  328 / 331).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:43.612639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342659Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 387 (commit 99640e41): DistributedHttpClient::{get,post} now race inner call against cx-cancel watcher (50ms poll). Mid-flight cancel surfaces as Ok(Err(Cancelled)) in ~70ms. Tick-380 snapshot test tightened to assert the new fast-cancel behavior (elapsed < 1s, exact Cancelled variant).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cx-cancel","distributed","http"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-l9mxa","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:58.856854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-lapw8","title":"Restore mux split wire JSON proptests dropped by 193a9ebb","description":"Commit 193a9ebb ("frankenterm-gui: saturate resize increment geometry") unintentionally deleted unrelated mux wire roundtrip coverage from frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs. The removed tests were split_direction_json_roundtrip and split_size_json_roundtrip at current worktree lines 210-221. Those tests were added one minute earlier by ccaa7385 and exercise SplitDirection/SplitSize serde JSON roundtrips. This is a cross-surface regression: a GUI commit should not reduce mux serialization coverage. Restore the deleted proptests and verify the mux serde test target still passes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:31.861035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:38.190544Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:38.190265Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in HEAD via commit a3409685 (2026-04-20). Both split_direction_json_roundtrip and split_size_json_roundtrip are present at frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:224-236. The test file has a pre-existing compile issue (PaneNode: Clone trait bound + 15-tuple prop_map) that is unrelated to the 193a9ebb deletion; filing that separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-lnlg4","title":"[reality-gap] tui/ftui_stub.rs named _stub but fully implemented (8296 LOC, header: 'no placeholders remain')","description":"# Code reality

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs`:
  - 8296 lines (non-trivial)
  - Header comment line 14: "All view bodies (Home, Panes, Search,
    Help, Events, Triage, History, Timeline) are backed by live
    state — no placeholders remain."
  - `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/mod.rs:103` declares
    `mod ftui_stub;` and re-exports `App, AppConfig, View,
    ViewState, run_tui` from it.

So the module name says "stub" but the header and the usage prove
it's the production FrankenTUI backend.

# Why this is a reality-check gap

Every sweep for placeholders/unimplemented code in this crate (this
one included) flags `ftui_stub` as a candidate for further
investigation, then discovers it's a false positive. Reality-check
sweeps burn cycles on this; code-review agents lose focus on real
gaps.

The naming is a legacy from when the file genuinely was a stub. The
content has grown into the real backend. The file name didn't
follow.

# Recommended disposition

```bash
git mv crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs \
       crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_backend.rs
```

…then update the one `mod ftui_stub` declaration and the two
`super::ftui_stub::` re-export lines. 15-second edit, preserves
git history via `git mv`.

# Risk

- `git mv` preserves blame + history.
- No external dependency on the file name (it's a sibling module).
- Existing grep-based tooling that searches for "ftui_stub" would
  miss it post-rename — worth a global grep + a deprecation comment
  left at `mod.rs` during the rename window.

# Severity

Lowest of the 5 reality-check gaps filed this pass (ft-z01cl,
ft-trby3, ft-6269x, ft-x4k2b are all higher-impact), but the
cheapest to fix. Good first-issue material.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:45:46.543886Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:51:39.224077Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hygiene","reality-gap","tui"]}

- Existing grep-based tooling that searches for "ftui_stub" would

Lowest of the 5 reality-check gaps filed this pass (ft-z01cl,

Good first-issue material.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:45:46.543886Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:54:09.847543Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:54:09.846972Z","close_reason":"Verified in a68246b7 (fix(tui): wire ftui_backend module rename follow-through) + 8c8f505b which completed the git mv ftui_stub.rs → ftui_backend.rs and updated mod.rs/keymap.rs/rollout.rs references.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hygiene","reality-gap","tui"]}
 {"id":"ft-lokam","title":"[LOW] diag bundle environment.json leaks absolute workspace cwd; tracing fields leak operator paths","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #6 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
Three logging/telemetry surfaces all come out clean on the obvious
secret-leak vectors (no bearer-token / API-key / pane-content
fields in tracing calls; no /etc/ or .ssh/ reads in diag
collection). One defense-in-depth gap remains: path privacy.

## Findings

### F1 [LOW] diag bundle environment.json includes absolute cwd
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic.rs:87-89
  ```rust
  cwd: std::env::current_dir()
      .ok()
      .map(|p| p.display().to_string()),
  ```
- Diag bundles (`ft diag bundle`) are often attached to bug reports
  and shared outside the host. An absolute cwd reveals:
  - the user's home path segment (`/Users/<username>/...` or
    `/home/<username>/...`),
  - the project path (`/Users/alice/projects/secret-startup-pitch-deck/`),
  - on some setups, mount points that hint at filesystem layout.
- Severity: LOW. Users implicitly share the workspace when
  debugging, but the current behavior removes the choice.

### F2 [LOW] tracing fields log operator-private paths
- Multiple `tracing::[info|warn|debug]!(..., path = %X.display(), ...)`
  call sites scatter socket paths, DB paths, and workspace paths
  through structured logs. Representative hits (non-exhaustive):
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:258, 302, 331,
      362, 401 — unix socket paths.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:1118 —
      `info_span!("telemetry_store_open", path = %db_path.display())`.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:1375, 2658, 2814 —
      socket paths on accept failures.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs:265 —
      `meta_path = %self.meta_path.display()`.
- Severity: LOW. These logs land in the user's local log file,
  not network-visible by default, but they do end up in diag
  bundles when `collect_recent_log_lines` is called.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- Bearer tokens in structured logs: grep across
  `tracing::{info,debug,warn,error}!` call sites for
  `token|bearer|secret|password|api_key` returned zero production
  hits. Clean.
- Pane content in tracing fields: no production code logs
  `segment.content`, `delta_text`, or `pane_text`. Only test
  `assert_eq!(segment.content, ...)` references found. Clean.
- Diag bundle reading privileged files: no `/etc/`, `.ssh/`,
  `.aws/`, `.gnupg/` reads in `diagnostic.rs`. The bundle only
  reads the ft SQLite DB (already redacted via `redact_events`)
  and writes to the workspace-local `diag_dir`. Clean.
- Environment variables in diag bundle: `gather_environment`
  captures only OS / arch / rust_version / wa_version / cwd —
  no `std::env::vars()` dump. Clean.
- Summarize_config: the docstring already calls out "no paths or
  secrets" and the implementation confirms — only numeric/boolean
  config values are copied over. Clean.

## Fix direction
- F1: replace absolute cwd with just the basename, or gate
  inclusion behind `DiagnosticOptions.include_cwd: bool` default
  `false`. Concretely at diagnostic.rs:87:
  ```rust
  cwd: if opts.include_cwd {
      std::env::current_dir().ok().map(|p| p.display().to_string())
  } else {
      std::env::current_dir()
          .ok()
          .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
  },
  ```
- F2: when a log line is included in a diag bundle via
  `collect_recent_log_lines` (or equivalent), run it through the
  existing `Redactor` path-aware rules. Non-bundle in-process logs
  stay as-is since they aid operator debugging on the local box.

## Severity rationale
- LOW (P2): neither finding reveals a secret, and both sit behind
  an operator action (`ft diag bundle` + sharing it). The fix
  shrinks the unintentional-share footprint but doesn't close a
  live exploit.

## Related
- ft-ybtyg (598a7c13) — snapshot/checkpoint hashing honesty.
- ft-2guux (doc reality-check) — complementary; this bead is the
  diagnostics-exfil counterpart.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:48:38.534325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:53:28.901879Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:53:28.901341Z","close_reason":"Closed by e790f481. F1 (cwd leak) fixed: DiagnosticOptions gains include_full_cwd:bool (default false). gather_environment now routes through new scrub_path_to_basename helper which returns only the final path component; opt-in via include_full_cwd:true restores absolute path for legitimate mount-point/multi-host repro cases. Both production entry points (generate_bundle + generate_bundle_with_cx) forward opts. 8 regression tests cover basename-only default, root/empty fallback to <scrubbed> sentinel, relative paths, opt-in restore, and the strongest guarantee: default environment.json output cannot contain /Users/ or /home/ segments. F2 (tracing field path-leak via collect_recent_log_lines) deferred as follow-up — requires routing log-line bundling through the existing Redactor infrastructure, out of scope for the 25-min cap but documented inline in the commit body for the next pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","diagnostics","logging","privacy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-ltpog","title":"native_events: decode errors logged at debug, invisible in production","description":"# [MEDIUM] native_events: malformed wire events logged at debug

**Subsystem:** native_events / protocol_recovery
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs (`handle_connection_with_cx`)

## The hole

When wire-event decoding fails (invalid JSON, bad base64, unknown event
type), the handler emitted:

\`\`\`rust
Err(err) => {
    debug!(error = %err, "failed to decode native event (cx path)");
}
\`\`\`

A malformed wire event is a protocol anomaly — version skew between
frankenterm and the mux-server, on-wire corruption, or a hostile peer
writing into the native-events socket. Operators routinely filter
`debug!` in production, so the signal was effectively lost.

Contrast with the `MAX_EVENT_LINE_BYTES` path a few lines above, which
already emits at `warn!`. Same class of anomaly, inconsistent level.

## Fix

Promoted the decode-error log to `warn!`. The `warn!` import was already
present — no dependency change.

Future work (separate bead): attach a telemetry counter for
decode-failure rate so it can drive a SLI rather than relying solely on
log sampling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:39.950825Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568595Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native-events","observability"]}
 {"id":"ft-m1zkw","title":"[MEDIUM] mux PaneNode serde proptests omit the recursive Split variant","description":"Audit finding from commits 095ca88a + ccaa7385.

Files:
- frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:108-113
- frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:3907-3914

`PaneNode` is a recursive wire type used directly by the codec and it has three variants: `Empty`, `Leaf`, and `Split { left, right, node }`. The current generator only produces `Empty` and `Leaf`:

```rust
fn arb_pane_node() -> impl Strategy<Value = PaneNode> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(PaneNode::Empty),
        arb_pane_entry().prop_map(PaneNode::Leaf),
    ]
}
```

So `pane_node_json_roundtrip` never exercises the recursive split-tree encoding path. The follow-up commit ccaa7385 added scalar roundtrips for `SplitDirection` and `SplitSize`, but that still does not cover nested `PaneNode::Split` layouts or the interaction with `SplitDirectionAndSize`.

Expected fix:
- make `arb_pane_node()` recursive and generate bounded split trees
- assert roundtrip for at least one non-trivial nested split shape

Regression risk: codec-facing tab topology serde can regress on split trees while the current proptests stay green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:43.315765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:27.500875Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:27.500567Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by fef393d1 (ft-n38nt) — arb_pane_node() now uses prop_recursive(3, 16, 2) generating bounded split trees, plus nested split shape fixture test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","mux","review","serde","test-gap"]}
